frameworks.name: activity-silent working memory
frameworks.one_line: WM held in connectivity changes, read out by neutral impulse perturbation.

frameworks.name: sensory recruitment of working memory
frameworks.one_line: Sensory cortices maintain modality-specific WM content during the delay.

frameworks.name: impulse-response perturbation paradigm
frameworks.one_line: Task-neutral stimulus pings the network to reveal hidden WM states.

core_question: Is the impulse-driven readout of working memory unique to vision, or does it generalize across and between sensory modalities?
key_assumptions: WM content can be silent and decoded only via perturbation
Task-neutral impulses do not themselves encode the memorandum
Modality-specific sensory cortices contribute to WM maintenance
manipulations: Memorandum modality (visual orientation vs auditory pure tone)
Impulse modality (visual vs auditory) during the maintenance delay
key_findings: Auditory impulse during auditory WM elicited a content-dependent EEG response
Visual impulse during auditory WM also elicited a content-dependent response
During visual WM, only visual impulses produced a content-specific response
interpretations: Impulse-driven WM readout generalizes to audition, supporting activity-silent maintenance
Visual cortex participates in auditory WM, but auditory cortex does not reciprocally support visual WM
